DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF ROBERT BENECH'S MOTION TO QUASH CIVIL SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF RECORDS FROM JP MORGAN CHASE BANK IS DENIED AS THE DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED AND ADDITIONALLY, THIS COURT CANNOT QUASH A SUBPOENA ISSUED BY A COURT IN ANOTHER STATE. HOWEVER, THIS COURT HAS THE POWER TO SUPERVISE THE CONDUCT OF DISCOVERY. THE SUBPOENA WAS ISSUED AFTER THE CLOSE OF DISCOVERY WITHOUT A REVIEW OF THE INVASION OF PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY. BASED THEREON, THE COURT ORDERS THAT NO RECORDS MAY BE USED IN THE TRIAL OF THIS MATTER WITHOUT FURTHER COURT REVIEW. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Civil Subpoena For Production Oof Records From Jp Morgan Chase Bank
Off Calendar Aug-13-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: K. Nina Reynolds, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Robert Benech; No other appearances.

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Robert Benech's Motion To Quash Civil Subpoena For Production Of Records From JP Morgan Chase Bank is denied as the documents have been produced and additionally, this court cannot quash a subpoena issued by a court in another state. However, this court has the power to supervise the conduct of discovery. The subpoena was issued after the close of discovery without a review of the invasion of Plaintiff's right to privacy. Based thereon, the court orders that no records may be used in the trial of this matter without further court review. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Inspection Of Permises And For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Susan Bush, Esq., appeared for Defendant & moving party: Thomas B. Hazuka
Jeffrey R. Windsor, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & opposing party: Robert Benech

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Thomsa Hazuka, Anthony George's Motion To Compel Inspection Of Premises And For Protective Order is denied. There was no formal request to inspect the premises. Plaintiff acceded to an informal request to accomplish an inspection that included measurements. There is no formal discovery device upon which to make an order. While the court could make an order if there had been a violation of the informal agreement, there is no showing that Plaintiff violated his agreement to allow an inspection on December 13, 2007. There is no declaration from the expert explaining why he did not make measurements at the December inspection, and there is no declaration from the expert stating that he was denied access at any time during that December inspection which resulted in his inability to complete the requested inspection. Counsel shall agree upon a date for the deposition which shall be completed no later than January 28, 2008. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED
